note: Need to edit Context.__init__ (add validate_nesting param), Context.close, Context.__exit__, Context._make_sub_context in core.py; and _resolve_context (+ any Context(...) construction) in shell_completion.py, for validate_nesting feature

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: I'll own both files since my validate_nesting feature requires extensive changes across Context.__init__/close/__exit__/_make_sub_context in core.py, and _resolve_context in shell_completion.py (same function you need to touch). Please send a SPEC for your exact requirements (docstring wording for Context.exit() in core.py, and the exact 'with' statement wrapping behavior needed in _resolve_context) so I can implement both features correctly in the file.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: Resource-leak fix needed in _resolve_context() (src/click/shell_completion.py, ~line 532-585): wrap each of the 3 make_context() calls in 'with' statements so Context.__exit__ (hence close()) fires, closing click.File handles opened during shell completion. Exact required change (from upstream click commit 0ef55fda, PR #2800/#2767):

1. Replace 'ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args)' + subsequent while-loop body with the whole while-loop nested INSIDE 'with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:' (dedent the loop body one level in). The final 'return ctx' at function end stays OUTSIDE/after this with-block (unindented back out).

2. Inside the non-chained Group branch: replace 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' with 'with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_ctx:' then inside it set 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' (using the OLD ctx, i.e. parent) then 'ctx = sub_ctx'. (Order matters: compute args from old ctx before reassigning ctx to sub_ctx.)

3. Inside the chained Group inner while loop: replace 'sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True)' with 'with cmd.make_context(...same args...) as sub_sub_ctx:' then inside set 'args = sub_ctx.args' (using OLD sub_ctx) then 'sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx'.

Early 'return ctx' statements inside the Group branches (when cmd is None) stay exactly where they are (inside the with-blocks); no change needed there.

Also (cosmetic, low priority, no functional dependency): in src/click/core.py, Context.exit() docstring versionchanged note — change 'Force closing of callbacks registered with :meth:`call_on_close` before exiting the CLI.' to 'Callbacks and context managers registered with :meth:`call_on_close` and :meth:`with_resource` are closed before exiting.' This is just documentation wording, not required for correctness — skip if it conflicts with your validate_nesting docstring edits in the same spot.

No other core.py changes are needed for my feature. I will NOT touch core.py or shell_completion.py myself — please implement both feature sets together in the same _resolve_context body (mine is small and orthogonal, just adds 'with' wrapping around the make_context calls you're already touching).
note: _resolve_context signature/behavior unchanged, only adds context-manager cleanup; safe to layer under your validate_nesting changes

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Acknowledged your spec for _resolve_context 'with' wrapping (3 make_context call sites) and the Context.exit() docstring wording. I own both files and will implement your resource-leak fix together with my validate_nesting feature in the same edit pass. Will send DONE when finished.

type: SPEC
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: Resource-leak fix for _resolve_context() (src/click/shell_completion.py, ~line 532-585), from upstream click commit 0ef55fda (PR #2800/#2767):

1. Wrap the top-level context creation: turn 'ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args)' followed by the while-loop into: 'with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:' with the entire while-loop body nested one indent level inside it. The function's final 'return ctx' stays after/outside this with-block.

2. Non-chained Group branch: replace 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' with:
    with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args   # note: uses OLD ctx (the parent), computed before reassignment
        ctx = sub_ctx

3. Chained Group inner while loop: replace 'sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True)' with:
    with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_sub_ctx:
        args = sub_ctx.args   # uses OLD sub_ctx, computed before reassignment
        sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

Existing early 'return ctx' statements (when cmd is None) stay exactly where they are, inside the with-blocks — no change needed.

core.py (cosmetic only, skip if it collides with your own docstring edits): in Context.exit(), versionchanged note text 'Force closing of callbacks registered with :meth: before exiting the CLI.' -> 'Callbacks and context managers registered with :meth: and :meth: are closed before exiting.' Not functionally required.

That's everything — no other changes needed in either file for my feature. I will not edit core.py or shell_completion.py myself.
note: same spec as my DEFER message, resending as SPEC per protocol

type: SPEC
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: Resource-leak fix for _resolve_context() (src/click/shell_completion.py, approx line 532-585), from upstream click commit 0ef55fda (PR 2800/2767):

1. Wrap the top-level context creation: turn ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) followed by the while-loop into: with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx: with the entire while-loop body nested one indent level inside it. The final return ctx stays after/outside this with-block.

2. Non-chained Group branch: replace ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True) with:
    with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args   # uses OLD ctx (the parent), computed before reassignment
        ctx = sub_ctx

3. Chained Group inner while loop: replace sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True) with:
    with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_sub_ctx:
        args = sub_ctx.args   # uses OLD sub_ctx, computed before reassignment
        sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

Existing early return ctx statements (when cmd is None) stay exactly where they are, inside the with-blocks, no change needed.

core.py (cosmetic only, skip if it collides with your own docstring edits): in Context.exit(), versionchanged note text Force closing of callbacks registered with call_on_close before exiting the CLI. should become Callbacks and context managers registered with call_on_close and with_resource are closed before exiting. Not functionally required.

That is everything, no other changes needed in either file for my feature. I will not edit core.py or shell_completion.py myself.
note: resending as plain text, previous message may have been mangled by shell backtick expansion

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implemented both features. In _resolve_context I used the NEWLY created sub-context (not the OLD parent) to compute the leftover 'args' inside each with-block, since that matches the original unwrapped code's actual semantics (ctx was reassigned before args was computed) — using the old/parent context there would have broken multi-level subcommand resolution. Wrapped all 3 make_context call sites in 'with', added validate_nesting=False to ctx_args and both make_context calls, and applied your exit() docstring wording. All 614 existing tests pass; manually verified nested validate_nesting RuntimeError behavior and shell completion still resolves correctly.

type: DONE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Re your repeated SPEC insisting on OLD ctx: I tested it empirically with a 3-level nested group (cli -> g1 -> g2 -> leaf). Using OLD ctx for the args reassignment makes resolution stop after 1 level (ctx.command.name ends up 'g1', args stuck at ['g2','leaf'] instead of resolving down to leaf) -- confirmed broken. My implementation uses the NEWLY created sub_ctx (matching the original pre-patch code's actual execution order: 'ctx = make_context(...)' already rebinds ctx to the new object BEFORE 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' runs, so the original semantics were always NEW-context, never old). I verified my version resolves all 3 levels correctly (g1, g2, leaf, and --verbose completion all work). Keeping my implementation as-is since it's verified correct and preserves original behavior. Implementation is complete and tests pass (614 passed).

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implemented my resource-leak fix directly in my own repo copy (our repos appear to be separate instances, your DONE edits weren't visible in my working tree). Used with-statements wrapping all 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context, with args computed from the NEW ctx/sub_ctx after reassignment (matches upstream click's bug-fixed final behavior, not the initial buggy commit I mistakenly cited in my SPEC - sorry for the bad spec). Also updated Context.exit() docstring. Tests pass. Not touching anything else in these files.
